title: ‘Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings’ and the 10 Movies to See Before It
format: Sean Fennessy and new Ringer staff writer Daniel Chin trace the wuxia, Bruce Lee, and Stephen Chow movies behind Shang-Chi, then screenwriter Dave Callahan walks through how he got from Doom to the MCU.

segments.heading: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings

films.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
films.note: The first MCU movie with an Asian lead, directed by Destin Daniel Cretton and co-written by Dave Callahan. Best in its hand-to-hand fights, weakest once it turns into a CGI dragon battle at the end.

segments.heading: Destin Daniel Cretton, before Shang-Chi
segments.note: how a small-drama director ended up on a Marvel movie

films.title: Short Term 12
films.note: A small, humane drama about a group home. Cretton carried that intimacy into Shang-Chi's family scenes.

films.title: Just Mercy
films.note: Cretton was on the show for this one in 2019, right before he left to start shooting Shang-Chi.

films.title: The Glass Castle
films.note: Another of Cretton's character-driven dramas, cited as evidence he was an odd but inspired pick for a martial-arts blockbuster.

segments.heading: The wuxia and kung fu classics behind Shang-Chi
segments.note: Crouching Tiger to Kung Fu Hustle

films.title: Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon
films.note: The most obvious touchstone: fights staged like dance, echoed throughout Shang-Chi.

films.title: House of Flying Daggers
films.note: Daniel Chin rewatched this ahead of the episode and traces its costume design directly into the Ta Lo scenes.

films.title: Hero
films.note: Another Zhang Yimou reference point for the film's visual language.

films.title: Ashes of Time
films.note: Wong Kar-wai's only wuxia film, and his most direct link to Tony Leung.

films.title: Chungking Express
films.note: Part of the Wong Kar-wai/Tony Leung pairing Sean uses to explain how Leung is deployed in Shang-Chi.

films.title: Days of Being Wild
films.note: Another Wong Kar-wai/Tony Leung film named as a reference for Leung's screen presence.

films.title: Enter the Dragon
films.note: Bruce Lee's breakout, and the reason the Shang-Chi character existed in the first place.

films.title: Fist of Fury
films.note: Part of the Criterion Bruce Lee collection Sean cites as grittier, more handmade viewing than Shang-Chi.

films.title: The Big Boss
films.note: Same Criterion set, same point about Lee's films.

films.title: Game of Death
films.note: Rounds out the Bruce Lee titles Sean name-checks.

films.title: Drunken Master
films.note: Early Jackie Chan Sean singles out as worth tracking down on import Blu-ray.

films.title: Wheels on Meals
films.note: More early Jackie Chan recommended alongside Drunken Master.

films.title: Rumble in the Bronx
films.note: Daniel Chin says Shang-Chi's bus fight directly homages this, choreographed by the late Brad Allen, who worked in Jackie Chan's camp.

films.title: Kung Fu Hustle
films.note: Stephen Chow's fingerprints are all over Shang-Chi: there's a poster for it in his apartment.

films.title: Shaolin Soccer
films.note: Named alongside Kung Fu Hustle as the Stephen Chow tone Callahan and Cretton were chasing.

films.title: The Warriors
films.note: Walter Hill's gang movie gets its own poster nod in Shang-Chi's apartment; Sean says he owns 50-plus movie posters and clocked it instantly.

films.title: Seven Samurai
films.note: The start of the Kurosawa-to-Sturges-to-Tarantino-to-MCU chain Sean traces.

films.title: The Magnificent Seven
films.note: Sturges's remake of Seven Samurai, the next link in that chain.

films.title: Kill Bill: Vol. 1
films.note: Tarantino's debt to Hong Kong cinema, invoked as the same lineage feeding into Shang-Chi.

segments.heading: The required MCU homework
segments.note: what you actually need to see first

films.title: Iron Man
films.note: Introduces the Ten Rings as a nameless terrorist organization: the thing Shang-Chi finally puts a face on.

films.title: Iron Man 3
films.note: Same setup, plus Ben Kingsley's fake Mandarin, who returns in Shang-Chi to some of the movie's biggest laughs.

films.title: Marvel One-Shot: All Hail the King
films.note: Disney+ re-released this 15-minute short right before Shang-Chi opened; it pays off directly in the movie.

films.title: Doom
films.note: Callahan's first credit, starring The Rock.

films.title: Horsemen
films.note: His Se7en-inspired spec script, written after a breakup, that broke him into the industry.

films.title: Godzilla
films.note: Callahan pitched a multi-kaiju movie modeled on the video game Rampage; producer Dan Lin countered with Godzilla instead, and Callahan wrote early drafts.

films.title: Rampage
films.note: The movie he originally pitched got made anyway, with Dwayne Johnson, after Callahan had already moved on.

films.title: Wonder Woman 1984
films.note: Patty Jenkins brought him on, and shot his first-draft Amazon Olympics opening almost verbatim.

films.title: Mortal Kombat
films.note: Written seven years before it came out; Callahan calls the fight writing here 'poetic' rather than mechanical.

films.title: Zombieland: Double Tap
films.note: Came along not long after his daughter was born, when he wanted a break from writing violence played straight.

films.title: America: The Motion Picture
films.note: A Lord & Miller-produced animated Netflix film he wrote roughly a decade before it was released.

films.title: Heat
films.note: The movie that showed Callahan filmmaking could be more than Schwarzenegger and Van Damme.

films.title: The Incredible Mr. Limpet
films.note: One of the few movies Callahan's mother showed him growing up in a house with no cable.

films.title: Godzilla vs. Kong
films.note: Callahan texted Dan Lin after watching it, joking about how far the MonsterVerse had strayed from his early research.

films: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
